incorrects.description: Student selected 21: counting by 1s instead of recognizing the scale of 5

params.description: Numbers 15, 20, 25, 30 pulse on the axis to show the pattern.

steps.text: Look at the pattern on the axis. These numbers skip by 5s, not 1s. What number comes after 15?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30: skipped ahead by 10 instead of 5

params.description: Numbers 15, 20, 25 pulse to show the spacing between marks.

steps.text: Each step on this axis goes up by 5, not 10. From 15 to 20 is 5. From 20 to the next mark is another 5. What's 20 plus 5?

incorrects.description: Student selected 22: added 2 instead of following the scale pattern

params.description: All axis numbers from 0 to 20 pulse to show the consistent interval.

steps.text: This axis counts by 5s, just like you counted symbols. 5, 10, 15, 20, and then what? Keep counting by 5s.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis numbers highlight in sequence: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, showing the counting by 5s pattern.

steps.text: Let me show you. The axis counts by 5s the whole way up: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20. After 20 comes 25. That's how the scale works. The skip-counting is built into the side of the graph.
